complete_examples.overview: Canonical examples of the Feature Developer workflow in action, demonstrating the complete lifecycle from feature request to Pull Request readiness. All state tracking uses `agent-workspace/feature/state.json` — markdown files are strictly for human readability and subtask execution context.

example.name: adding_user_authentication
example.scenario: A developer wants to add user authentication (login, registration, JWT sessions) to an existing Express.js + React application. The project already has a populated agent-workspace/knowledge/ directory.
example.user_request: "I want to build a user authentication feature with login, registration, and JWT-based sessions."

step.approach: Check if agent-workspace/knowledge/ directory exists with all 4 core files. It does — architecture.md, database.md, api-routes.md, and frontend-ui.md are present. Proceed to Phase 1.

step.description: Phase 1: Initialization — Smart Resume & Branch Creation
step.approach: Check for agent-workspace/feature/state.json. None exists — fresh feature. Feature Developer uses ask_followup_question with Suggested Responses. User clicks "feature/user-auth". Run: git checkout -b feature/user-auth. Success. Feature Developer immediately creates agent-workspace/feature/state.json: { "feature_name": "User Authentication", "branch_name": "feature/user-auth", "git_automation_enabled": true, "smoke_test_automation": { "enabled": false, "environments": { "web": null, "cli": null, "native": null, "desktop": null }, "missing_prerequisites": [] }, "current_phase": 1, "plan_status": "draft", "total_steps": 0, "current_step": 0, "pivot_in_progress": false, "phase_5_substep": null } Feature Developer initializes TWO empty template files on disk using write_to_file: agent-workspace/feature/plan.md (populated with the 6-section Plan template) and agent-workspace/feature/reference.md (populated with the Reference template). Spawn Architect subtask with both templates and the Tiered Documentation Retrieval Protocol. Architect discusses feature with user, researches dependencies (e.g., bcrypt, JWT libraries), saves docs to agent-workspace/external-docs/, fills out both template files with the 6-section template, then stops for user approval. User approves. Feature Developer reads agent-workspace/feature/plan.md, counts 5 steps in Section 5 (counting EVERY heading that starts with `### [ ] Step`), updates state.json: "total_steps": 5.
step.expected_outcome: Branch created. state.json initialized with current_phase: 1, total_steps: 5. agent-workspace/feature/plan.md has 5 steps: Step 1: User model and DB schema, Step 2: Registration endpoint, Step 3: Login endpoint, Step 4: JWT middleware, Step 5: React login/register forms. agent-workspace/feature/reference.md populated with bcrypt and JWT snippets per step. agent-workspace/external-docs/bcrypt.md and external-docs/jsonwebtoken.md saved.

step.approach: Feature Developer reads both files. Syntax check passes (all 6 plan sections present, every step has a Live Smoke Test, H3 headings match). Spawns Critic subtask (architect mode) with the "strict Principal Engineer" prompt. Critic reviews for invisible steps, actionable smoke tests, code-snippet alignment, and architectural flaws. Critic makes minor polish edits, then stops for user approval. User approves. Feature Developer updates agent-workspace/feature/state.json: "plan_status": "validated", "current_phase": 3. Reads git_automation_enabled from state.json (true). Runs: git add agent-workspace/ && git commit -m "docs: finalize validated plan, reference, and external docs"
step.expected_outcome: state.json now has plan_status: "validated", current_phase: 3. Both files committed. Proceed to Phase 3.

step.approach: Feature Developer reads state.json: current_step is 0, so starts with Step 1. Reads "Step 1: User Model and DB Schema" from agent-workspace/feature/plan.md Section 5. Standard task → spawn code mode. Reads git_automation_enabled from state.json (true) → uses Instruction 4 variant with git commands. Prompt includes: project context, feature context, step details, the reference_materials instruction (to read agent-workspace/feature/reference.md for bcrypt snippets), and critical instructions. Subtask completes: creates user model, migration, tests pass, live smoke test confirmed (user runs SQL query to verify table), git commit made. Subtask reports: "Created User model with fields: id, email, password_hash, created_at. Database table: users. Password hashing uses bcrypt."
step.expected_outcome: Feature Developer marks Step 1 [x] in markdown (human readability). Updates Shared Technical Context (Section 6) in agent-workspace/feature/plan.md with: "- User model: id, email, password_hash (bcrypt), created_at. Table: users." Increments current_step in state.json to 1. Commits plan+state changes: git add && git commit -m "docs: update feature implementation plan state" current_step (1) < total_steps (5), so loops back to Phase 3 for Step 2.

step.approach: Continue the loop for Steps 2-5. Each step: - Read current_step from state.json, locate the corresponding step in the markdown plan - Read git_automation_enabled from state.json to select Instruction 4 variant - Spawn appropriate mode (code for all, as each is well-defined) - Include accumulated Shared Technical Context from Section 6 - Include the reference_materials instruction to read agent-workspace/feature/reference.md - After completion: extract new context, append to Section 6 of agent-workspace/feature/plan.md, mark step [x] in markdown, increment current_step in state.json

Spawns Architect to update the partitioned knowledge base: - User model schema → agent-workspace/knowledge/database.md - Auth API endpoints → agent-workspace/knowledge/api-routes.md - JWT auth pattern → agent-workspace/knowledge/architecture.md - AuthContext + login/register forms → agent-workspace/knowledge/frontend-ui.md Finalizes state.json in-place: current_phase = 5, plan_status = "archived". Archives plan as agent-workspace/feature/archive/plan-feature-user-auth.md. Archives reference as agent-workspace/feature/archive/ref-feature-user-auth.md. Archives state.json as agent-workspace/feature/archive/state-feature-user-auth.json. agent-workspace/external-docs/ is explicitly preserved (never moved or deleted). agent-workspace/knowledge/ is explicitly preserved (never moved or deleted). Reads git_automation_enabled from archived state.json (true) → runs final git commit. Announces completion.
step.expected_outcome: Partitioned knowledge base updated with new auth patterns across all relevant files. state.json finalized in-place first, then all three files (plan, reference, state.json) archived together. agent-workspace/external-docs/ and agent-workspace/knowledge/ directories preserved for future features. User notified: "Feature branch feature/user-auth is complete and ready for a Pull Request!"

key_takeaways.takeaway: state.json drives all loop/progress logic; markdown checkboxes are only for humans
Every step produces a testable outcome with a Live Smoke Test
Shared Technical Context (Section 6) grows organically as each step completes
The Feature Developer never writes code, only orchestrates and tracks state
Clean Code Refactor is enforced at every step, preventing technical debt
The partitioned knowledge base separates architectural, database, API, and UI concerns

example.name: smart_resume_from_state_json
example.scenario: The user was in the middle of executing a feature when their session was interrupted. On reconnect, the Feature Developer must resume exactly where it left off using state.json.

step.approach: Feature Developer checks for agent-workspace/feature/state.json. It exists. Reads state.json: { "feature_name": "Shopping Cart", "branch_name": "feature/shopping-cart", "git_automation_enabled": true, "current_phase": 3, "plan_status": "validated", "total_steps": 4, "current_step": 2, "pivot_in_progress": false } Interpretation: current_phase is 3, plan_status is "validated", current_step (2) <= total_steps (4). This means the plan is approved and Steps 1-2 are complete, Step 3 is next.
step.expected_outcome: Feature Developer proceeds directly to Phase 3, reads Step 3 from agent-workspace/feature/plan.md, and spawns the next code subtask. No re-validation, no re-planning.

key_takeaways.takeaway: state.json enables clean, deterministic resume without fragile markdown parsing
The combination of current_phase, plan_status, and current_step uniquely identifies the resume point
No magic strings like [ARCHITECT_DRAFT_COMPLETE] are needed

example.name: handling_major_pivot
example.scenario: During Step 3 (Login endpoint), the subtask reports a MAJOR PIVOT REQUESTED: the user wants to switch from JWT to OAuth 2.0.

step.approach: Subtask returns with "MAJOR PIVOT REQUESTED". Feature Developer updates agent-workspace/feature/state.json: - "plan_status": "draft" - "current_phase": 1 - "total_steps": 0 (CRITICAL: must be 0 so resume logic knows Architect is drafting). (Leave current_step as 2, since Steps 1 and 2 are done).
step.expected_outcome: Feature Developer spawns Architect with pivot context. It explicitly instructs the Architect to preserve the completed [x] steps and only rewrite the pending [ ] steps for OAuth 2.0.

step.approach: Architect rewrites the remaining steps. Feature Developer re-counts total steps using the TOTAL count logic (counting EVERY heading that starts with `### [ ] Step` OR `### [x] Step` — e.g., now 6). Updates state.json: "total_steps": 6. Routes back through Phase 2 (Syntax check → Critic review). Critic validates the revised plan. User approves. Feature Developer updates state.json: "plan_status": "validated", "current_phase": 3. Resumes execution Phase 3. Reads current_step (2), so it starts working on Step 3.
step.expected_outcome: Plan adapted cleanly. Execution continues seamlessly from where it left off.

example.name: handling_roadblock
example.scenario: During Step 3 (Login endpoint), the subtask discovers the existing user service uses a different password hashing approach than what was designed in Step 1.

step.approach: Subtask reports: "The existing user service uses argon2, but Step 1 implemented bcrypt. I need guidance on which approach to use."
step.expected_outcome: Feature Developer asks the user: "The subtask found a conflict between the new bcrypt implementation and the existing argon2 usage. Should we: 1) Standardize on argon2 (update Step 1's implementation) 2) Keep bcrypt for the new auth flow 3) Let the Architect decide?"

step.approach: User chooses option 1. Feature Developer updates agent-workspace/feature/state.json: sets "plan_status" to "draft", "current_phase" to 1, and "total_steps" to 0 (CRITICAL: must be 0 so the Smart Resume logic knows the Architect is still actively drafting). Preserves "current_step" as-is — already-completed steps are valid and the Architect will only rewrite pending steps. Feature Developer spawns Architect to revise the plan: preserve completed [x] steps (Step 1 and Step 2) and rewrite/update pending steps to use argon2 instead of bcrypt. Architect revises the plan, potentially adding a Step 1b. Feature Developer reads the revised plan, re-counts TOTAL steps (counting EVERY heading that starts with `### [ ] Step` OR `### [x] Step`), and updates state.json with the new "total_steps".
step.expected_outcome: Plan now includes Step 1b. state.json has total_steps reset to 0 then re-counted after Architect finishes. Feature Developer routes the revised plan through Phase 2 (Critic review) to validate the new architecture before execution.

step.approach: Critic reviews the revised plan for consistency with the new argon2 approach. User approves. Feature Developer updates state.json: plan_status = "validated", current_phase = 3. Resumes execution: spawns code mode for Step 1b, then continues with Step 3.
step.expected_outcome: Plan validated after revision. Execution continues with corrected implementation.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Roadblocks trigger user consultation, not autonomous decisions
The plan is a living document that adapts as new information emerges
Architect can be re-invoked to revise completed steps if needed
When new steps are added, update total_steps in state.json
All plan revisions — even minor ones — should route through Phase 2 Critic review before execution
